title: Iterable vs Iterator
beginnerExplanation: An Iterable is a collection of items that you can loop over (like a list or a tuple). An Iterator is the helper agent that actually does the work of fetching the items one by one.
intermediateExplanation: Iterables define the __iter__ method which returns an Iterator. Iterators implement the __next__ method which yields elements and raises StopIteration when depleted. Iterators are stateful—once consumed, they are exhausted.
advancedExplanation: Iterables are compatible with collections.abc.Iterable. Iterators subclass collections.abc.Iterator and must implement both __iter__ (returning self) and __next__.

realWorldAnalogy.title: The Book and the Reader
realWorldAnalogy.description: An Iterable is like a physical Book (the pages exist, but they don't turn themselves). An Iterator is like a Reader (the person turning page-by-page, holding their current spot with a finger).

mentalModel: Iterables contain the data, but Iterators contain the state (index cursor) of the loop.
visualDiagram: Iterable (Book) -> iter() -> Iterator (Reader with pointer) -> next() -> values
executionWalkthrough: 1. iter() is called on the list container.
2. An iterator object is created with its cursor initialized at index 0.
3. next() is called on the iterator, returning the current element and incrementing the index.

memoryState.note: Holds data but not current iteration cursor

commonMistakes: Trying to reset an iterator (it cannot be reset; you must create a new one).

debuggingMission.scenario: A player tries to loop over an iterator a second time, but gets no elements.
debuggingMission.brokenCode:
nums = [1, 2]
it = iter(nums)
list(it)
list(it)  # Empty!
debuggingMission.explanation: Iterators are exhausted on first consumption. Always generate a fresh iterator for reuse.

practiceQuestions.question: Which method must a custom Iterable class implement to return an Iterator?
practiceQuestions.explanation: Iterables must implement the __iter__ method to build and return their iterator helper.

challengeQuestions.question: Which exception is raised when next() runs out of items?

projectExercise.title: Verify Iterable Interfaces
requirements: Use isinstance to verify if list is an Iterable.
projectExercise.starterCode:
from collections.abc import Iterable
print(isinstance([], Iterable))

bossBattle.name: Sentinel of the Stream
bossBattle.description: Bypass the Sentinel's checks by passing stateful iterators.
bossBattle.mechanic: Feed lazy sequences to prevent memory explosion.

complexityAnalysis.timeComplexity: O(1) to create an iterator, O(1) per step to fetch the next item.
complexityAnalysis.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space, as it only holds a cursor reference.
complexityAnalysis.description: Iterators avoid duplicating the source collection in memory.

memoryImpact.allocationBytes: 104 bytes per iterator object, irrespective of the source list size.
memoryImpact.optimizationNotes: Always prefer iterators over lists when streaming millions of DB rows.

performanceNotes: Calling next() has low overhead, but calling it millions of times in Python can be slower than native C loops.
